decks.title: Friends · The One With Ross's New Girlfriend
decks.summary: 基于 S02E01 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: I know you did.
cards.exampleSentence: Ugh. I'm just gonna deal with it. I'm just gonna deal with it
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 啊。我只是要处理一下。我只是去处理它
cards.nextSentence: I gotta get out of here!
cards.sceneContext: 成年人的生存法则，面对难缠的亲戚或突如其来的生活烂摊子，只能硬着头皮去处理。
cards.usageTip: 无敌高频短语。“应对/处理”。表达束手无策时，常说 I don't know how to deal with this.

cards.previousSentence: Before I say anything...
cards.exampleSentence: ...is this one of those things where you break up with a guy.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: ......这是你与一个男人分手的事情之一。
cards.nextSentence: Then I tell you what I think...
cards.sceneContext: 剧中最揪心的反复分手与复合。分手在这个朋友圈里是个永恒且极具杀伤力的话题。
cards.usageTip: 动词词组，接对象时必须带介词 with (break up with him)。如果只是两人谈话，直接说 we broke up。

cards.previousSentence: - But... - Go, go, go! Come on, come on!
cards.exampleSentence: Okay, so, uh, what did you find out?
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 好吧，那么，呃，你发现了什么？
cards.nextSentence: He said...
cards.sceneContext: 朋友间隐藏的八卦或极其敏感的情感秘密意外走漏风声。
cards.usageTip: 强调经过调查或者突然“得知真相”。如果别人的秘密败露，可以用 He was found out.

cards.previousSentence: But there was always this voice inside that kept saying:
cards.exampleSentence: "It's never gonna happen. Move on. " You know whose voice that was?
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: “这永远不会发生。继续前进。”你知道那是谁的声音吗？
cards.nextSentence: God?
cards.sceneContext: 苦苦哀求或深陷失恋泥淖的人，最后必须面对让生活翻篇的无情现实。
cards.usageTip: 无论事业还是感情，当一页翻过，生活必须继续时用到。

cards.previousSentence: Is this juice?
cards.exampleSentence: Whoa. And the fact that you dumped him because he hit on Phoebe?
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 哇哦。你因为他勾搭菲比而抛弃了他？
cards.nextSentence: Oh, God, I know. I'm pathetic, I'm a loser.
cards.sceneContext: 在纽约夜生活中，陌生人带有明显暗示的搭讪和调情，往往是引发剧情吃醋的导火索。
cards.usageTip: 明确带有性暗示或追求意味的接触。区别于普通聊天 chat。

cards.previousSentence: Oh, my God.
cards.exampleSentence: Oh, my... Excuse me. Move, move, move! Emergency, please!
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 哦，我的……对不起。动起来，动起来，动起来！紧急情况，请！
cards.nextSentence: Excuse me, excuse me.
cards.sceneContext: 在这段对话场景里，该用语深刻切中了人物之间关于生存、成长或是打破情感僵局的核心冲突脉络。
cards.usageTip: 美剧高频词。切忌单独背诵其字面意思，强烈建议结合所在句子的搭配进行全句沉浸式复述以掌握口语精髓。

cards.exampleSentence: So, we should catch up.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我们应该好好聊聊近况。
cards.sceneContext: 老朋友好久不见或发生大事后试图同步信息的开场白。
cards.usageTip: 通常和 with someone 连用，如 catch up with you。

decks.title: Friends · The One With The Breast Milk
decks.summary: 基于 S02E02 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: Oh, I tasted Ben's milk and Ross freaked out.
cards.exampleSentence: I... I did not freak out.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我……我没有惊慌。
cards.nextSentence: - Why'd you freak out? - Because it's breast milk.
cards.sceneContext: 面对意外怀孕或突发灾难时彻底崩溃。这也是二十几岁青年面对成年规则的第一反应。
cards.usageTip: 极其重要的情绪词。比 scared 或 panic 更能形容“完全失控、吓得抓狂”的状态。

cards.previousSentence: Ladle?
cards.exampleSentence: Will you guys grow up?
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 你们会长大吗？
cards.nextSentence: This is the most natural, beautiful thing in the world.
cards.sceneContext: Monica父母或强势亲戚对年轻人常常进行的居高临下的说教。
cards.usageTip: 命令别人理智点时说：Grow up! (成熟点吧！)。带有一丝谴责其幼稚的意味。

cards.previousSentence: - So. - So.
cards.exampleSentence: I just thought the two of us should hang out a bit.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我只是觉得我们两个应该出去玩一下。
cards.nextSentence: I mean, you know, we've never really talked.
cards.sceneContext: 老友文化的核心基石：什么都不做，就是在某地纯粹地和死党耗时间干瞪眼。
cards.usageTip: 最无负担的社交词。提出 hang out 意味着没有浪漫企图，就是哥们儿/闺蜜间瞎玩。

cards.previousSentence: - Hey, thanks for your jacket. - Oh, no problem.
cards.exampleSentence: - You can borrow it, by the way. - Heh.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: - 顺便说一句，你可以借用它。 - 呵呵。
cards.nextSentence: - Oh, here are your keys, honey. - Thank you.
cards.sceneContext: 说话人试图不经意地转移话题或补充重要信息。常常带着假装漫不经心但其实很在意的心理战。
cards.usageTip: 高频缩写词 BTW。一般放在句首或句中，作过渡用。能让口语显得极其自然。

cards.exampleSentence: Oh, that is just gross.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 简直太恶心了。
cards.sceneContext: 对于母乳喂养等引起剧中人（尤其是Joey等直男）心理不适的滑稽指控。
cards.usageTip: 极其地道的感叹词，比 disgusting 更常用、更幼稚一点。

decks.title: Friends · The One Where Heckles Dies
decks.summary: 基于 S02E03 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: This is what I do.
cards.exampleSentence: I'm gonna end up alone, just like he did.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我最终会孤独终老，就像他一样。
cards.nextSentence: Chandler, Heckles was a nut case.
cards.sceneContext: 描述原本计划好的事情完全偏离轨道，结果却落得一个极其荒诞的下场。
cards.usageTip: 常接动名词 (end up doing) 或介词 (end up in jail)，指“最终落得……的结局”。

cards.previousSentence: Such as?
cards.exampleSentence: Like crop circles or the Bermuda Triangle or evolution.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 就像麦田怪圈、百慕大三角或进化论。
cards.nextSentence: Whoa, whoa, whoa.
cards.sceneContext: Ross作为古生物学家与Phoebe关于唯物主义“进化论(evolution)”的爆笑辩论。展现了科学与信仰的冲撞。
cards.usageTip: 达尔文进化论的专属词。形容个人或产业发展时，也可译为“演变/沿革”。

cards.previousSentence: What if I never find somebody? Or worse, what if I found her...
cards.exampleSentence: ...but I dumped her because she pronounces it "supposably"?
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: ......但我甩了她，因为她发音为“可能”？
cards.nextSentence: Chandler, come on, you're gonna find somebody.
cards.sceneContext: Joey把supposedly（假定地）读错成的假词。讽刺了没文化却喜欢装出文化分子样子的常态。
cards.usageTip: 发音错别字！这是 supposedly 被没文化的人误读的版本，朋友若这么说会被嘲笑。

cards.previousSentence: Pheebs, I've studied evolution my entire adult life.
cards.exampleSentence: And I can tell you, we've collected fossils from all over the world...
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我可以告诉你，我们收集了来自世界各地的化石......
cards.nextSentence: ...that actually show the evolution of different species.
cards.sceneContext: Ross日夜痴迷的恐龙化石(fossils)。在朋友看来这是非常枯燥且书呆子的专业代表。
cards.usageTip: 化石。引申用来嘲笑某人思想陈旧、是个老古董 (You're a fossil)。

cards.exampleSentence: He drank too much and passed out.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 他喝太多直接昏睡过去了。
cards.sceneContext: 关于老友聚会中极度疯狂且失去知觉的爆笑丑态。
cards.usageTip: 相比 faint（虚弱晕倒），pass out 更多用于醉酒、精疲力尽后的死睡。

decks.title: Friends · The One With Phoebe's Husband
decks.summary: 基于 S02E04 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: Um, I'm gonna go meet Duncan. He's skating tonight at the Garden.
cards.exampleSentence: He's in the Capades.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 他在卡帕德斯。
cards.nextSentence: - The Ice Capades? - No, no, the Gravel Capades.
cards.sceneContext: 代指 Ice Capades。这是美国一种非常通俗甚至是针对带小孩家庭的冰上滑冰秀，对成年人来说常常显得很幼稚。
cards.usageTip: 有专有文化属性的词条。看到此词能意会这是一种“合家欢式的冰上表演”即可。

cards.previousSentence: Oh, as opposed to your other multifunctional nipples?
cards.exampleSentence: I can't believe you. You told me it was a nubbin.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我不敢相信你。你告诉我那是个小东西。
cards.nextSentence: Joey, what did you think a nubbin was?
cards.sceneContext: Chandler身体上神秘的第三个乳头。成为整季大家用作调侃男权软肋或是怪癖的笑料。
cards.usageTip: 原意指小凸起、发育不全的玉米穗等。剧中特指第三乳头，是极其罕见的生僻医学术语幽默化表达。

cards.exampleSentence: Why would you turn down that job?
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 你居然连那个工作都拒绝？
cards.sceneContext: 面对诱人高薪的工作机会或者求偶行为，反而采取清高的态度去回绝。
cards.usageTip: Turn someone down 是一种稍微委婉且带着遗憾的分手或拒绝行为。

decks.title: Friends · The One With Five Steaks And An Eggplant
decks.summary: 基于 S02E05 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: Julie. Okay, yes, I see how you got there.
cards.exampleSentence: You ever figure out what that thing's for?
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 你知道那东西是做什么用的吗？
cards.nextSentence: No, see, I'm trying this screening thing.
cards.sceneContext: 角色们往往面临生活的重新洗牌与职业抉择，极需在混乱中理出一条出路。
cards.usageTip: 日常高频替代 think 或是 solve。意为“搞懂、相通、解决”。

cards.previousSentence: ...she's probably gonna realize, hey, you're not Bob.
cards.exampleSentence: I am hoping that when Bob doesn't show up...
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我希望当鲍勃没有出现时...
cards.nextSentence: ...she will seek comfort in the open arms of the wry stranger at the next table.
cards.sceneContext: 形容某人意外在场，或者答应的事情没有出现(didn't show up)。
cards.usageTip: 不请自来或者露面。比 arrive 听起来更加口语化和生动。

cards.previousSentence: Gift? The thing's not the gift?
cards.exampleSentence: No, the thing was we were gonna go see Hootie and the Blowfish.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 不，问题是我们要去看看胡蒂和河豚。
cards.nextSentence: Hootie and the... Oh, my... I can catch them on the radio.
cards.sceneContext: 提到了一支非常有名的90年代乐队 Hootie & the Blowfish。借此展现主角们的文化品味与时代烙印。
cards.usageTip: 字面意思是河豚。这是不可数形式或者单复数同型的词，在这里属专有名词，了解美国90年代流行文化即可。

cards.exampleSentence: Don't take it out on me!
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 别拿我当出气筒！
cards.sceneContext: 一方生活极度不顺回家对无辜室友发邪火，合租生活的真实痛点。
cards.usageTip: 常用结构 take something out on someone。

decks.title: Friends · The One With The Baby On The Bus
decks.summary: 基于 S02E06 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: Let go of my hand!
cards.exampleSentence: Good plan. Next time we want to pick up women...
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 好计划。下次我们想泡妞的时候...
cards.nextSentence: ...we should go to the park and make out.
cards.sceneContext: 在酒吧或派对试图搭讪并带走异性回家，Joey的拿手绝活之一。
cards.usageTip: 在酒吧语境下就是“泡妞/搭讪成功”。日常也指接电话或捡起东西。

cards.previousSentence: ...I got, like, $1.75.
cards.exampleSentence: But then "Smelly Cat"? Oh.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 那么“臭猫”呢？哦。
cards.nextSentence: I got 25 cents and a condom.
cards.sceneContext: Phoebe神曲《臭臭猫 Smelly Cat》，代表了她童年阴影与对底层社会的深切共情。
cards.usageTip: 有难闻气味的。在文化上与猫连在一起，已经是不可磨灭的荧屏经典IP了。

cards.previousSentence: That's what's gonna kill me.
cards.exampleSentence: Ahem. I'm allergic to kiwi.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 咳咳。我对猕猴桃过敏。
cards.nextSentence: No, you're not. You're allergic to lobster and peanuts and...
cards.sceneContext: Ross对某些食物或物品过敏(allergic)。用过敏来制造身体不适的危机感是情景喜剧的常用笑点。
cards.usageTip: 常用搭配 be allergic to something。不仅可以指生理过敏，口语中也能夸张地表达极度排斥某人某事（如 I'm allergic to hard work）。

cards.exampleSentence: I forgot my asthma inhaler.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我忘了带哮喘吸入器。
cards.sceneContext: 对于带婴儿或者身体羸弱的人群面临突发危机时的慌张。
cards.usageTip: 发音特殊，th 往往不发音或弱化。注意：/ˈæzmə/。

decks.title: Friends · The One Where Ross Finds Out
decks.summary: 基于 S02E07 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: Chandler, I'm unemployed, in dire need of a project.
cards.exampleSentence: Wanna work out? I can remake you.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 想锻炼身体吗？我可以重塑你。
cards.nextSentence: Uh, I would, but that might get in the way of my lying-around time.
cards.sceneContext: 无论是试图维持一段注定崩盘的感情还是某个绝妙的计划行不通时体面的说辞。
cards.usageTip: 万金油短语：1. 解决问题；2. 健身运动；3. 两人感情开花结果。

cards.previousSentence: Look, I've been through a divorce. Trust me, you're gonna be fine.
cards.exampleSentence: You can't see it because you haven't had closure...
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 你看不到它，因为你还没有关闭......
cards.nextSentence: Aah!
cards.sceneContext: Rachel为了彻底对Ross死心，试图去寻找一种心理学上的“解脱/了结”(closure)。
cards.usageTip: 极其重要的西方情感术语。指分手、悲剧后的“彻底翻篇与释怀”。Get closure 意味着不再对过去藕断丝连。

cards.previousSentence: Ow. Ow. Ow. Ow. Ow.
cards.exampleSentence: She's insane! The woman is insane.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 她疯了！女人疯了。
cards.nextSentence: It's before work, after work, and during work.
cards.sceneContext: 用来形容某个前任的行为或者某种疯狂的主意简直不可理喻。
cards.usageTip: 口语极高频词，可以译为“疯了”。遇到惊讶的事也可以说 That's insane! (太疯狂了)。

cards.exampleSentence: Just let go of the past.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 过去的事就让它过去吧。
cards.sceneContext: 在难以忘怀前任或旧习时朋友发出的最后心理通牒。
cards.usageTip: 除了物理上的撒手，在西方心灵学说中指灵魂的解脱。

decks.title: Friends · The One With The List
decks.summary: 基于 S02E08 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: - Yeah. - Cool.
cards.exampleSentence: All right, check out this bad boy!
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 好吧，看看这个坏孩子！
cards.nextSentence: Twelve megabytes of RAM, 500-megabyte hard drive...
cards.sceneContext: 角色在咖啡馆或酒吧看上了某人，或者去调查某个奇异事件。
cards.usageTip: 用法极多：可以指酒店退房、超市结账，更常用在社交里指“去瞅瞅/打量某人或某物” (Check her out)。

cards.previousSentence: Uh, Mr. Rastatter, what does this job entail? The ad wasn't clear.
cards.exampleSentence: Mockolate.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 莫科拉特。
cards.nextSentence: - I'm sorry? - Mockolate.
cards.sceneContext: Monica帮一家公司研发的人工滞销代可可脂“Mockolate”。深刻揭示了万恶的资本食品工业的虚假营销。
cards.usageTip: 造词法(Mock虚假 + chocolate巧克力)。学习这种把词拼在一起的幽默感即可。

cards.exampleSentence: Let's make a list of pros and cons.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我们列个优缺点清单吧。
cards.sceneContext: Ross为在两个女人间做决定而使用这套理性工具，结果招致毁灭性后果。
cards.usageTip: 常和 make a list 连用，典型的美式实用主义脑回路。

decks.title: Friends · The One With Phoebe's Dad
decks.summary: 基于 S02E09 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: And I guess he doesn't live in a hut in Burma where there's no phones?
cards.exampleSentence: Last I heard, he was a pharmacist somewhere upstate.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我上次听说，他是北部某地的一名药剂师。
cards.nextSentence: That makes no sense.
cards.sceneContext: 在这段对话场景里，该用语深刻切中了人物之间关于生存、成长或是打破情感僵局的核心冲突脉络。
cards.usageTip: 美剧高频词。切忌单独背诵其字面意思，强烈建议结合所在句子的搭配进行全句沉浸式复述以掌握口语精髓。

cards.previousSentence: Okay, you're whiny...
cards.exampleSentence: ...you are obsessive, you are insecure...
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: ……你很执着，你缺乏安全感……
cards.nextSentence: ...you're gutless. - Heh.
cards.sceneContext: 形容Monica那种无可救药的控制狂以及对清洁整理有着极强执念的性格缺陷。
cards.usageTip: 如果你有强迫症(OCD)，你就是 obsessive-compulsive。常用来形容人神经质的执着。

cards.exampleSentence: Now I'm stuck with him.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 现在我被他赖上了。
cards.sceneContext: 因为一时的善良或意外导致不得不长时间忍受极其讨厌的室友/亲戚。
cards.usageTip: 表示被迫承受无法摆脱的困境。

decks.title: Friends · The One With Russ
decks.summary: 基于 S02E10 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.exampleSentence: Are you guys going to make up?
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 你们到底准不准备和好？
cards.sceneContext: 情侣间经过剧烈狗血吵架之后，外人急切探听他们是否还会走回一起。
cards.usageTip: 除了化妆，make up with someone 是两性关系最核心用语之一。

decks.title: Friends · The One With The Lesbian Wedding
decks.summary: 基于 S02E11 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: If she were marrying a guy, you wouldn't expect me to go.
cards.exampleSentence: If she were marrying a guy, she'd be the worst lesbian ever.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 如果她嫁给一个男人，她将成为有史以来最糟糕的女同性恋。
cards.nextSentence: Did I miss it?
cards.sceneContext: 剧中Ross前妻开启的女同伴侣线。代表了当时影视剧试图接纳多元家庭结构的尝试。
cards.usageTip: 专指女同性恋。发音重音在最前。比 homosexual 更口语、自然。

cards.exampleSentence: He is such a pain in the ass.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 他真是个烦死人的家伙。
cards.sceneContext: 用略带粗俗的俚语来形容那些在派对或者婚礼上阴魂不散、不断惹麻烦的人。
cards.usageTip: 非常不客气的口语。正式场合请谨慎使用。

decks.title: Friends · The One After The Super Bowl (1)
decks.summary: 基于 S02E12 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: Oh, please. I'm not allowed to make one joke in the "monkey is penis" genre?
cards.exampleSentence: Back up, please.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 请备份。
cards.nextSentence: Back up. Come on, come on.
cards.sceneContext: 在争吵或陈述事实时，让对方“退回去/重新说一遍刚才的话”(back up)。反映角色试图理清荒谬逻辑。
cards.usageTip: 除了形容汽车倒车或电脑数据备份，口语中常常在难以置信时说 “Wait, back up.” 让别人重头说。

cards.exampleSentence: Can't get the monkey off your back?
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 无法摆脱你背上的猴子吗？
cards.nextSentence: Then put it in your mouth...
cards.sceneContext: 在这段对话场景里，该用语深刻切中了人物之间关于生存、成长或是打破情感僵局的核心冲突脉络。
cards.usageTip: 美剧高频词。切忌单独背诵其字面意思，强烈建议结合所在句子的搭配进行全句沉浸式复述以掌握口语精髓。

cards.previousSentence: Then put it in your mouth...
cards.exampleSentence: ...with Monkeyshine Beer!
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: ...与 Monkeyshine 啤酒！
cards.nextSentence: Wow.
cards.sceneContext: 猴子Marcel回归好莱坞拍戏以及其引发的闹剧。这个词带着强烈的滑稽杂耍感。
cards.usageTip: 调皮捣蛋、滑稽的恶作剧。极少在正式英语中出现，属于俚语词汇。

cards.previousSentence: Oh, my God.
cards.exampleSentence: I got my very own stalker!
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我有自己的跟踪者了！
cards.nextSentence: - Hey, guys. Hey.
cards.sceneContext: 由于明星梦或误以为对方是剧中角色，遇到的狂热痴迷到变态跟踪的粉丝。
cards.usageTip: 跟踪狂/变态疯狂粉丝。社会新闻高频词，现常见于形容在社交平台上疯狂视奸别人主页的人。

cards.exampleSentence: Did you guys hook up?
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 你们俩发生故事了？
cards.sceneContext: 在超级杯或者是大明星客串集，大家极度八卦两人之间是否有浪漫火花。
cards.usageTip: 从暧昧到上床皆可用 hook up，具体尺度由上下文决定，极其高频。

decks.title: Friends · The One After The Super Bowl (2)
decks.summary: 基于 S02E13 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.exampleSentence: She really stands out in the crowd.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 她在人群中太惹眼了。
cards.sceneContext: 在试戏或聚会中，试图表现出碾压其他所有人的独特气质与企图心。
cards.usageTip: 作名词可写成 standout，指群体中最优秀的那一个。

decks.title: Friends · The One With The Prom Video
decks.summary: 基于 S02E14 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: Some girl ate Monica!
cards.exampleSentence: Shut up! The camera adds ten pounds.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 住口！相机增加了十磅。
cards.nextSentence: So how many cameras are actually on you?
cards.sceneContext: 当别人喋喋不休讨论尴尬问题时，情绪爆发喝令对方闭嘴。也用于极度惊讶的感叹。
cards.usageTip: 非常粗鲁。但年轻人听到天大八卦时说 Shut up! 则是相当于“别逗了/不会吧！”的超惊讶表达。

cards.previousSentence: - Eh? What do you say? - I don't know.
cards.exampleSentence: It's a bracelet.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 这是一个手镯。
cards.nextSentence: Isn't it? It's engraved too. Check it out.
cards.sceneContext: Joey送给Chandler一条极其浮夸的土豪金手链(俗称“友谊手链”)。这是全季表现直男审美的爆笑名场面之一。
cards.usageTip: 发音重音在最前。与 necklace(项链) 和 ring(戒指) 同属配饰词汇。

cards.previousSentence: Now how do you know that?
cards.exampleSentence: Because she's your lobster.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 因为她是你的龙虾。
cards.nextSentence: Oh, she's going somewhere.
cards.sceneContext: Phoebe著名的“龙虾理论”：龙虾终身只有一个伴侣。用来暗示Ross和Rachel是彼此的天作之合。
cards.usageTip: 文化限定词条。受老友记影响，You're my lobster 成了“你是我的灵魂伴侣”的地道浪漫情话。

cards.previousSentence: Oh, she's going somewhere.
cards.exampleSentence: It's a known fact that lobsters fall in love and mate for life.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 众所周知，龙虾会相爱并终生交配。
cards.nextSentence: You know what? You can actually see old lobster couples...
cards.sceneContext: 呼应上述“龙虾理论”，指代永远牵手同行的一对老夫老妻。
cards.usageTip: 见 lobster 的用法。作为复数时可形容恩爱的终身伴侣。

cards.previousSentence: - No, but... - No! Listen to me!
cards.exampleSentence: I fell for you and I get clobbered.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我爱上了你，但我却被击垮了。
cards.nextSentence: You then fall for me and I again get clobbered.
cards.sceneContext: Ross和玩伴打橄榄球等对抗活动时，被描述为狠狠击败或暴打(clobbered)。体现男性的好胜自尊。
cards.usageTip: 口语化的“被暴打/被彻底击败”。比 beat 或 defeat 更生动、更带画面感。

cards.exampleSentence: We are watching the prom video.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我们在看高中毕业舞会的录像带。
cards.sceneContext: 这集通过展现肥胖Monica和爆炸头Rachel的高中黑历史反讽当代审美。
cards.usageTip: Prom 是美国高中文化最最狂热的一环，代表了青春期的全部自尊。

decks.title: Friends · The One Where Ross And Rachel...You Know
decks.summary: 基于 S02E15 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: "You're sure?" "Yeah, I'm fine. "
cards.exampleSentence: I gotta set up the music. I got a new CD changer.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我得把音乐调好。我有一个新的 CD 换碟机。
cards.nextSentence: Of course, the divorce only left me with four CDs to change.
cards.sceneContext: 被盲人相亲或是安排尴尬的约会局，是都市未婚青年挥之不去的相亲梦魇。
cards.usageTip: 可以作名词 setup。在约会文化里，set someone up 意思是“为某人介绍对象”。

cards.exampleSentence: All right, no peeking. No peeking.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 好吧，不许偷看。不准偷看。
cards.nextSentence: No peeking. No peeking!
cards.sceneContext: 偷看对面的丑陋裸男(Ugly Naked Guy)是Friends长期的固定梗，满足了人猎奇的偷窥欲。
cards.usageTip: 偷偷看一眼。比 look 或 see 更带有一点猥琐或隐秘的好奇心。

cards.exampleSentence: I will never give in.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我绝对不会认输的。
cards.sceneContext: 老友因芝麻绿豆小事爆发冷战，谁也不愿先低头的一种自私却真实的心理博弈。
cards.usageTip: 比 surrender (投降) 更小气一点，强调不再倔强。

decks.title: Friends · The One Where Joey Moves Out
decks.summary: 基于 S02E16 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: Speaking of whom...
cards.exampleSentence: ...I hear he's got some 20-year-old twinkie in the city.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: ...我听说他在城里有个20岁的小伙子。
cards.nextSentence: Finger cramp! Oh, God!
cards.sceneContext: 极其高糖分且劣质但深受喜爱的美式海绵小蛋糕。用以讽刺穷困时期廉价的美式加工食品。
cards.usageTip: 特拉华小蛋糕(奶油夹心)。由于保质期惊人且满是防腐剂，常被调侃为核爆留下的唯一食物。

cards.previousSentence: What?
cards.exampleSentence: The spoon! You licked and you put.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 勺子！你舔了然后放了。
cards.nextSentence: You licked and you put.
cards.sceneContext: 剧中用来夸张形容被某些难以解决的情况击败，或是真正被狗舔了。具有喜剧双关意味。
cards.usageTip: 除了舌头舔，口语中 have a problem licked 表示“彻底解决了某件难事”。

cards.exampleSentence: Are you going to move in together?
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 你们准备同居了吗？
cards.sceneContext: 感情进展到这一步，是对年轻人丧失单身自由的一记重锤和极大考验。
cards.usageTip: 常接 with (move in with her)，是美剧恋爱线分摊房租和感情试金石的名场面。

decks.title: Friends · The One Where Eddie Moves In
decks.summary: 基于 S02E17 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: Damn it, Ross. Get your butt out of the bathroom.
cards.exampleSentence: Calm down. I'm blow-drying.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 冷静下来。我正在吹干。
cards.nextSentence: Blow-drying what? You have no hair.
cards.sceneContext: 角色面对前伴侣或是极大压力时陷入失控，被旁人安抚。常常安抚不成反被吼。
cards.usageTip: 在极度愤怒的情境下说这个词非常危险，容易激怒对方。建议在非正式和小吵闹时使用。

cards.exampleSentence: That guy is so creepy.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 那家伙看起来瘆得慌。
cards.sceneContext: 形容Eddie那种极度变态、半夜盯着人看但又自认和蔼可亲的室友病态感。
cards.usageTip: 用于描述使人不寒而栗、极其不舒服的人或环境。

decks.title: Friends · The One Where Dr. Ramoray Dies
decks.summary: 基于 S02E18 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: Okay, you know, that sounded so much better in my head.
cards.exampleSentence: Eddie! I didn't sleep with your ex-girlfriend.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 艾迪！我没有和你前女友睡过
cards.nextSentence: That's exactly what someone who slept with her would say.
cards.sceneContext: Ross一直无法妥善处理和前女友的关系，前任永远是美剧主角感情线上最大的变数之一。
cards.usageTip: 前任的通用构词法：ex + 身份。可以单说 my ex，表示我的前男/女友。

cards.exampleSentence: We have to kick him out.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我们必须得把他轰出去。
cards.sceneContext: 终于受不了神经病室友，试图用尽手段夺回领地主权的惨烈行动。
cards.usageTip: 常说 get kicked out of someplace (被某地赶出来)。

decks.title: Friends · The One Where Eddie Won't Go
decks.summary: 基于 S02E19 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.exampleSentence: I really messed up this time.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我这次算是彻底搞砸了。
cards.sceneContext: 成年人常常在面临重要抉择或工作时失误，带有一种懊悔和沮丧的哀叹。
cards.usageTip: 如果你想说某人精神状态不好，可以说 he is messed up。

decks.title: Friends · The One Where Old Yeller Dies
decks.summary: 基于 S02E20 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.exampleSentence: I can't put up with his behavior.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我再也忍受不了他的所作所为了。
cards.sceneContext: 当一个人突破了你的底线（如Richard抽雪茄或者室友的恶习）时发出的反抗宣告。
cards.usageTip: 比 bear (忍耐) 更加口语化，强调忍受让人恼火的人或事。

decks.title: Friends · The One With The Bullies
decks.summary: 基于 S02E21 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.previousSentence: - No. - You're kidding.
cards.exampleSentence: It was ridiculous. These guys were bullies.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 这太荒谬了。这些家伙是恶霸。
cards.nextSentence: Actual bullies.
cards.sceneContext: Ross和Chandler在中央公园被两个恶霸(bullies)占座抢劫。将成年人拉回童年被欺负的窘境制造反差感。
cards.usageTip: 名词 bully(恶霸) 的复数，作动词时意为“霸凌”。校园霸凌就是 school bullying。

cards.exampleSentence: Are you intimidated by him?
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 你是不是觉得在他面前有点自卑害怕？
cards.sceneContext: 面对极其自信霸道或者地位高的人，心生畏怯不敢表现真实的自己。
cards.usageTip: 这是一个表现权力关系不对等时极常用的心理学词汇。

decks.title: Friends · The One With The Two Parties
decks.summary: 基于 S02E22 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.exampleSentence: Why would you bring that up?
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 你为啥非要重提那件破事？
cards.sceneContext: 聚会中最怕的就是朋友不过大脑地突然爆出某人的往日黑料。
cards.usageTip: 不要和 raise/grow up (抚养)混淆。这里特指引发一个不想谈的话题。

decks.title: Friends · The One With The Chicken Pox
decks.summary: 基于 S02E23 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.exampleSentence: I have chicken pox.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 我出水痘了。
cards.sceneContext: 极其滑稽的反差：已经成熟的大人在关键的浪漫约会时却得了小孩子的传染病。
cards.usageTip: 病理名词，发音简单但十分生活化。发冷、发痒 (itchy) 是常伴的形容词。

decks.title: Friends · The One With Barry And Mindy's Wedding
decks.summary: 基于 S02E24 真实字幕提取，覆盖中级学习者可复用的高价值词汇与短语。

cards.exampleSentence: How did the audition go? - Incredible.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 试镜进行得怎么样？ - 极好的。
cards.nextSentence: I met the director. You'll never believe who it was.
cards.sceneContext: Joey为了演艺事业进行试镜工作。试镜是所有纽约底层演员角色的核心生存挑战。
cards.usageTip: 演员专属词汇。除了作名词，也可以直接作动词 (audition for a role)。

cards.exampleSentence: It's time to make a move.
cards.exampleSentenceTranslation: 是时候该出手了。
cards.sceneContext: 在季终关键时刻，鼓励主角抛开所有优柔寡断，去追求真爱或跨过最后防线。
cards.usageTip: 在酒吧指走过去搭讪，在感情里指主动亲吻或者表白决断。
